attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches in final 200m on pan-flat finishes. Occasionally attacks long (bunched sprint after early move) when tactical situation favors energy conservation.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on leadout train execution and positioning reconnaissance. Team often stays anonymous until final 500m, then exploits rival leadout collapses. Multiple instances of leadout disintegration (Vuelta 2021-2022) indicate fragility under pressure.
terrain_preference: Exclusively pan-flat coastal or technical city finishes. Exposed and dropped on any sustained climbing: 7.1% grades eliminate him by 15km to go. Rolling Ardennes-style terrain drops him at 8km.
vulnerability: Cannot survive even minor sustained climbs on lumpy sprint stages (dropped 4+ times before final 15-20km). Positioning failures in Grand Tours suggest either fitness deficits or team coordination breakdown when fatigued. Mechanical fragility under pressure.
key_patterns: Dropped on any climb >7% gradient with 15+ km remaining, confirmed across Giro and Vuelta (5 instances)
Wins require either reconnaissance-based positioning advantage or rival leadout collapse (3 documented wins)
Leadout train disintegrates in Grand Tours: missing from sprints despite team control (Vuelta 2021-2022 pattern)
Struggles maintaining position behind own leadout in stage races, indicating fitness degradation over 3 weeks
Technical finish positioning failures contrast with coastal/straight sprint success (Paris-Nice, UAE Tour divergence)
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 UAE Tour and Paris-Nice show restored sprint execution and positioning intelligence after 2022 struggles, suggesting one-week race specialization
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 8
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3
2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 7
